sections.about: This metric measures women KMPs as a percentage of total KMPs. It falls under BRSR Section A — General Disclosures, Participation/Inclusion/Representation of women; it is not an Essential or Leadership Indicator. A higher value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: SAIL led in FY2022-23 at 5.00% because it disclosed 1 female KMP out of 20. By FY2023-24 and FY2024-25, all four companies reported 0.00%, including Tata, JSW and Jindal across the full period. This indicates that Board-level diversity is not flowing into the statutory KMP layer; Jindal can sustain 27.28% female Board representation in FY2024-25 while still reporting 0 female KMPs.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2024-25, Tata is tied at rank 1 at 0.00% because all peers are also at 0.00%; there is no numerical gap, but this is a weak absolute outcome. Tata’s rank improved only mechanically after SAIL’s decline from 5.00% to 0.00%. For internal management, the relevant action area is succession planning for statutory KMP roles, not peer-rank optics.
sections.targets: No targets identified for this metric.
